results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE PURPOSE OF THIS PROJECT IS TO IMPROVE HOW REAL-LIFE EXPOSURE TO MULTIPLE CHEMICALS IS CONSIDERED IN TOXICITY ASSESSMENTS OF CHEMICAL MIXTURES.ACTIVITIES:RESEARCHERS WILL IDENTIFY REAL-LIFE CHEMICAL MIXTURES FROM BIOLOGICAL SAMPLES TAKEN DURING A LARGE EPIDEMIOLOGIC BIRTH COHORT STUDY. THE RESEARCH TEAM WILL THEN PRIORITIZE EXPOSURE MIXTURES USING EPIDEMIOLOGIC ANALYSIS TO IDENTIFY MIXTURES MOST STRONGLY ASSOCIATED WITH CHILD HEALTH. RESEARCHERS WILL ALSO DEVELOP MIXTURE TOXICITY ASSESSMENTS FOR PRIORITIZED EXPOSURES. SUBRECIPIENT:THE SUBAWARD TO CLEMSON UNIVERSITY WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR AIM 3, WHICH INVOLVES TESTING THE TOXICITY OF THE CHEMICAL MIXTURES USING CELL LINES. THE GOALS ARE TO FIRST SCREEN INDIVIDUAL CHEMICALS TO ASSESS WHETHER THEY CAN CAUSE OR INHIBIT ADIPOGENSIS. THEN, RESEARCHERS WILL DEVELOP A SET OF TRANSFECTED MESENCHYMAL STEM CELLS THAT HAS FIVE DIFFERENT RECEPTORS OF INTEREST TO TEST WHETHER THE SELECTED CHEMICALS BIND TO THEM, AND DETERMINE THE POTENCY OR STRENGTH OF THE BINDING. FINALLY, THE CLEMSON LAB WILL USE THE PRIORITIZED CHEMICALS AND MIXTURES FROM AIMS 1 AND 2 TO EXAMINE ALTERED ADIPOCYTE FORMATION AND TRIGLYCERIDE DEPOSITION, WHILE SIMULTANEOUSLY EXAMINING MECHANISTIC EFFECTS IN THE TRANSFECTED CELLS.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES INCLUDE ANNUAL AND FINAL REPORTS AS WELL AS AN INNOVATIVE FRAMEWORK FOR TOXICOLOGICAL ASSESSMENT OF REAL-WORLD EXPOSURE MIXTURES ACROSS A BROAD RANGE OF BIOLOGICAL ENDPOINTS. THE RESULTING TOOLS MAY PROVIDE A VALUABLE RESOURCE TO RISK ASSESSORS AND THE BROADER SCIENTIFIC COMMUNITY INTERESTED IN RISK ASSESSMENT OF CHEMICAL MIXTURES. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROGRAM INCLUDE CHILDREN'S HEALTH, HUMAN HEALTH, THE ENVIRONMENT, AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC.
